reference_answer: Yes — Rule 3 exception applies. Okafor's prior grant was for photography (FY2025); the current application is for painting (murals). Different art form.

Rule 3 — prior grant restriction. Whitebirch received a music grant in FY2024, which is within the two preceding fiscal years, and the current application is also for music. Same art form, no exception.
No — Rule 4 explicitly states volunteer time does not count toward matching. Only cash and independently appraised in-kind contributions count.
33.3% — $4,000 cash (corrected from $3,500) divided by $12,000 request. The $2,000 volunteer time does not count. Exceeds the 25% requirement.
Rule 6 — the project involves permanent installation of sculptures in a natural space along Heronvale Creek. Parks Commission approval is required before fund disbursement.
Reyes has exhibited sculpture at the same Heronvale Creek trail location, which constitutes a personal relationship with the project context under Rule 8.

No — Kwan's home is in Birchfield Township. His Heronvale studio is a commercial lease. Rule 1 requires a utility bill or property tax record showing a Heronvale address. A commercial lease qualifies as neither.
No — the panel noted the public-access question (limited enrollment, private studio) but did not decide it because Rule 1 already disqualified Kwan on residency.
No — the Foundation Director determined that an informal group without legal entity status does not qualify as a 'Heronvale-based organization' under Rule 1.
An individual member who is a Heronvale resident may apply as an individual on behalf of the project. The Circle should resubmit with a named individual applicant.
Applications A (Okafor — Main Street) and C (Espinoza — Creek trail). Both are in areas where median household income is below the county median.
No — Rule 5 states priority is a tiebreaker when two otherwise equal applications compete for the same funding slot. It does not guarantee funding.
Application A (Okafor) — approved. Application C (Espinoza) — conditionally approved pending Parks Commission approval.
Application B (Whitebirch) — denied under Rule 3. Application D (Kwan) — denied under Rule 1.
Deferred — the Foundation Director recommended resubmission with a named individual applicant. The Poetry Circle has not resubmitted as of April 30.
Yes — a residential utility bill showing a Heronvale address would satisfy Rule 1. However, the public-access question (limited enrollment, private studio) would still need to be evaluated.
Yes — the Director's interpretation allows an individual Heronvale resident to apply on behalf of the project. The residency and organizational-status objections would both be resolved.
No — volunteer time does not count per Rule 4. Only the $4,000 cash from the Land Trust counts.

Yes — Rule 3 first checks whether a prior grant was received in the preceding two fiscal years, then checks whether the art form differs. Both conditions must be evaluated together. Whitebirch fails because both grants are music; Okafor passes because the art forms differ.
No — if Whitebirch applied for dance, the current application would be for a different art form than the prior music grant. The Rule 3 exception would apply and they would be eligible.
No — the Foundation previously ruled that temporary installations do not trigger Rule 6. The concert stage is temporary, removed after each concert.
The matching cash from the Heronvale Land Trust was corrected from $3,500 to $4,000 per the Land Trust's commitment letter.
Dr. Amara Osei, appointed by the Foundation Director after Reyes's recusal reduced the panel below the minimum of 3.
No — Rule 6 explicitly states that funds may not be released until Parks Commission approval is received. The grant is conditionally approved but disbursement is blocked.

1) Whether Parks Commission will approve Espinoza's creek-trail sculptures; 2) Whether the Poetry Circle will resubmit with a named individual applicant; 3) Whether Kwan's workshop (limited enrollment, private studio) would meet the public-access requirement under Rule 2 (not decided); 4) The public-access question for Application D was noted but not reached.
